title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. praetermissa
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. praetermissa, also known as the pale helleborine, is a subspecies of the orchid family and a type of flowering plant. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. praetermissa include its potential to treat conditions such as diabetes, skin irritations, and digestive issues. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
